fall into place in the ribosome.
An enzyme called aminoacyl tRNA synthetase (not shown)
attaches amino acids to their corresponding tRNA
molecules using energy from ATP. Each amino acid has
its own tRNA molecule with the anticodon for that
When the ribosome reaches a stop codon,
The first tRNA drops off its amino
acid, breaks off and leaves to pick
up another amino acid. The second
moves over to make room for another tRNA.
